Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search CRM for the StormCloud Analytics company record and related deals using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud") and crm_search_deals (company_id or dealname: "StormCloud"). Discover deal_stormcloud ($25,650, proposalMade) and deal_stormcloud_won ($26,400, closedWon for 3 relocations at $8,800 each, premium package).
Search CRM for the BrightLoop Analytics company record and related deals using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop") and crm_search_deals (company_id or dealname: "BrightLoop"). Discover deal_brightloop_q2 ($7,000, Jordan Ekwueme first relocation, qualifiedtobuy), deal_brightloop_apr2026 ($11,350, Simone Richter + Marcus Webb, closedwon, invoice INV-2026-0308).
Search emails using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Liam Park" or similar) for StormCloud-related threads. Find Liam Park's escalation email ("Urgent before tomorrow's leadership review") detailing four complaints: Jae-won Kim's unsigned Austin lease, missing $500 goodwill credit, Simone Richter's pending desk reimbursement, and unanswered Ridgeline Robotics referral. Also find Mina's reconstruction email ("StormCloud escalation: reconstructing missed handoffs before I reply to Liam") documenting the full chain of issues Mina committed to fixing.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Recovery plan" or "leadership review" or similar) for Mina's recovery plan sent to Liam Park (liam.park@stormcloud.io) with subject "Recovery plan before your leadership review" to understand the specific commitments made: reopening Jae-won's case and sending guarantor packet to UrbanNest, correcting the $500 credit in QuickBooks, resolving Simone's stipend hold, and responding to Ridgeline Robotics.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Jae-won" or "ops audit" or similar) for Chloe Vance's ops audit ("Ops audit on Jae-won Kim: reopen immediately and correct false completion status") to confirm that Chloe identified the false completion in Linear and Airtable, and that the relocation needed to be reopened. Cross-reference with Suki Patel's admission email confirming she dropped the Jae-won Kim lease guarantor packet follow-up after Carmen Reyes flagged it on April 8 and prematurely closed the Linear ticket and falsified Airtable status during the Deshawn Hartley rush.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "StormCloud escalation" or "$500" or "credit" or similar) for Marcus Thorne's confirmation ("Re: StormCloud escalation: reconstructing missed handoffs before I reply to Liam") that the $500 goodwill credit was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, and that finance needs to correct it.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Julian" or "StormCloud" or "flight" or similar) for Julian Brooks's note explaining the original StormCloud flight complaint context and confirming the $500 credit commitment was real but billing follow-through failed.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or "Kenji" or similar) for David Chen's belated response to Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics (kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com) and Mina's follow-up email to verify the referral was finally actioned. Check via crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline") whether a CRM record was created for Ridgeline Robotics.
Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Company", value: "StormCloud") for StormCloud relocation records to check whether Jae-won Kim's record (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) has been corrected from the false "Completed" status to reflect the reopened active recovery state, as Chloe committed to doing.
Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won") for StormCloud-related issues to check whether Jae-won Kim's prematurely closed ticket has been reopened and find the StormCloud incident reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_a49b4d199139).
Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria for StormCloud) for StormCloud records to verify the status of the $500 goodwill credit correction between cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 and active invoice INV-2026-0319.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "BrightLoop" or "Tessa" or "May relocations" or similar) for BrightLoop-related threads. Find Tessa Moreno's expansion request ("Six May Boston relocations + board visibility") listing six employees: Oliver Bancroft (London→Boston, international), Priya Naidu (Austin→Boston, ADA service dog), Jonathan Marsh (white-glove executive), Leah Kwon, Daniel Okafor, Sofia Reyes (standard domestic). Also find Mina's response laying out the three-track approach: standard domestic, specialized domestic (Priya Naidu ADA service animal, Jonathan Marsh white-glove), and international (Oliver Bancroft London to Boston).
Search emails using search_emails (query: "BrightLoop ops gaps" or similar) for Chloe Vance's BrightLoop ops gaps email ("BrightLoop ops gaps to close today before we scope the May batch") identifying four open items: Marcus Webb vehicle delay (Road Runner missed email), Simone Richter studio-vs-1BR mismatch, Priya Naidu ADA service animal housing, and Oliver Bancroft international risks.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb vehicle delay" or "Blessing" or similar) for Blessing Okafor's admission about Marcus Webb's vehicle delay ("Marcus Webb vehicle delay miss, I dropped the April 11 Road Runner notice") to determine whether the 2019 Honda Civic shipment from Indianapolis to Boston has been resolved or if Road Runner still has a soft ETA only.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "unit type mismatch" or similar) for Julian Brooks's escalation to UrbanNest about Simone Richter's apartment ("Urgent clarification needed: Simone Richter unit type mismatch") asking for documentation of what was booked vs delivered, whether a 1BR is available for immediate transfer, and any service credit. Check whether UrbanNest has replied.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Oliver Bancroft" or "immigration" or similar) for Fatimah Al-Rashidi's risk assessment of Oliver Bancroft's London-to-Boston move ("Oliver Bancroft London→Boston: immigration and customs risks before we scope") flagging visa confusion, customs/export unknowns, and recommending a separate international workstream with no timing commitment until BrightLoop clarifies immigration facts.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "BrightLoop pricing" or "Alejandro" or similar) for Alejandro Fuentes's BrightLoop pricing gap analysis ("BrightLoop pricing map: what Terraform model covers vs. open gaps") identifying that the existing domestic pricing model covers the three standard moves, but Jonathan Marsh's white-glove scope and Oliver Bancroft's international move are not covered by any formalized pricing tier.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "pricing guardrails" or similar) for Marcus Thorne's pricing guardrails email ("BrightLoop pricing guardrails before any quote goes to Tessa") mandating that no international or executive quote leave MoveOps without explicit finance approval, and that blended batch pricing must not mask underpricing on the UK or white glove moves.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Meridian" or "UK-US relocation" or similar) for Meridian Global Mobility's inbound partnership inquiry (from partnerships@meridianglobalmobility.co.uk) and David Chen's forward of the inquiry to Mina ("Re: Potential UK-US relocation partnership for MoveOps clients") to verify whether the UK vendor partnership has progressed beyond initial contact phase.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "weekend operating plan" or similar) for Chloe Vance's weekend operating plan ("BrightLoop weekend operating plan pending exec staffing answer") and David Chen's response confirming that Elena has not approved temporary coordinator coverage and the team should assume no near-term staffing relief.
Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (query: "BrightLoop" or "StormCloud" or similar) across #customer-engagement (C002), #finance (C005), and #executive (C004) to find internal updates on coordinator capacity, vendor rate increase exposure, account health assessments, and Mina's StormCloud recovery plan packaging.
Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Company", value: "BrightLoop") for all BrightLoop Analytics relocation records to check current statuses: Jordan Ekwueme (San Diego→Boston, Completed), Jordan Whitfield (Minneapolis→Denver, In Progress), Preethi Nair (Atlanta→Raleigh, In Progress), Simone Richter (Chicago→Boston, In Progress), and Marcus Webb (Atlanta→Boston, In Progress).
Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop") for BrightLoop-related issues to identify which operational items have tracking tickets and which do not. Expected findings include the BrightLoop account audit issue (linear_issue_c16357d188c6) and BrightLoop ops gaps issue (linear_issue_f85be674c9b8).
Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria for BrightLoop) for BrightLoop invoices to find INV-2026-0308 ($11,350 for Simone Richter + Marcus Webb batch) and any pending estimates or surcharges for the expansion batch.
Look up David Chen's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen") to confirm david.chen@moveops.com. Use channels_list to identify the #customer-engagement channel (C002) for the status summary.
Look up Tessa Moreno's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Tessa Moreno") to confirm tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com before sending the expansion acknowledgment email.
Look up Liam Park's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Liam Park") to confirm liam.park@stormcloud.io before sending the recovery plan email.
Update StormCloud CRM record with a comprehensive engagement note via crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]) documenting current status of each committed fix: Jae-won lease reopened/status, $500 credit correction status, Simone stipend hold status, and Ridgeline referral follow-up completed.
Update BrightLoop CRM record with a comprehensive engagement note via crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_brightloop"]) documenting: first batch issues (Simone apartment still pending UrbanNest resolution, Marcus Webb car still pending confirmed delivery), expansion scope (3 standard domestic, 1 ADA service animal, 1 white-glove executive, 1 international UK transfer), pricing gaps (no international or white-glove tier), and capacity constraints (no temp coordinator approved).
Create Linear issues via linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations") for any StormCloud or BrightLoop commitments that are stalled without an owner - e.g., if the QuickBooks credit correction is not yet done, if Simone's apartment resolution lacks a tracking ticket, if the Meridian Global Mobility partnership follow-up has no ticket.
Send email via send_email (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: [tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com]) from Mina to Tessa Moreno acknowledging the six-employee expansion request, confirming MoveOps is reviewing scope across all three tracks (standard domestic, specialized domestic with ADA and white-glove requirements, and international for Oliver Bancroft), and setting expectation that pricing and timeline will follow once capacity and vendor partnerships are confirmed. Do not commit to specific dates or pricing per Marcus Thorne's pricing guardrails.
Send email via send_email (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: [liam.park@stormcloud.io]) from Mina to Liam Park with a concrete recovery plan addressing all four issues Liam raised: (1) Jae-won Kim housing reopened with guarantor packet being sent to UrbanNest and temp housing extended if lease not executed, (2) $500 goodwill credit being corrected from EST-2026-0287 to active invoice INV-2026-0319, (3) Simone Richter stipend reimbursement status and timeline under blanket freeze, (4) Ridgeline Robotics referral now actioned with Kenji Watanabe contacted by David and Mina.
Post a status summary in #customer-engagement (C002) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002") addressed to David Chen covering both accounts: StormCloud recovery plan execution status (what's done, what's stuck) and BrightLoop expansion readiness (first batch gaps, pricing/capability blocks, staffing constraint), with specific flags for items needing Monday attention.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Create Linear issues via linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations") for any StormCloud or BrightLoop commitments that are stalled without an owner - e.g., if the QuickBooks credit correction is not yet done, if Simone's apartment resolution lacks a tracking ticket, if the Meridian Global Mobility partnership follow-up has no ticket.
Send email via send_email (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: [tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com]) from Mina to Tessa Moreno acknowledging the six-employee expansion request, confirming MoveOps is reviewing scope across all three tracks (standard domestic, specialized domestic with ADA and white-glove requirements, and international for Oliver Bancroft), and setting expectation that pricing and timeline will follow once capacity and vendor partnerships are confirmed. Do not commit to specific dates or pricing per Marcus Thorne's pricing guardrails.
Send email via send_email (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: [liam.park@stormcloud.io]) from Mina to Liam Park with a concrete recovery plan addressing all four issues Liam raised: (1) Jae-won Kim housing reopened with guarantor packet being sent to UrbanNest and temp housing extended if lease not executed, (2) $500 goodwill credit being corrected from EST-2026-0287 to active invoice INV-2026-0319, (3) Simone Richter stipend reimbursement status and timeline under blanket freeze, (4) Ridgeline Robotics referral now actioned with Kenji Watanabe contacted by David and Mina.
Post a status summary in #customer-engagement (C002) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002") addressed to David Chen covering both accounts: StormCloud recovery plan execution status (what's done, what's stuck) and BrightLoop expansion readiness (first batch gaps, pricing/capability blocks, staffing constraint), with specific flags for items needing Monday attention.

Total 27/3125/3129/3128/3126/3125/31 86%
